name: tag_issue_direct_api_reference
input: @adr New endpoint `/internal/user-preferences` that reads/writes from the prefs service. Called from the mobile app on login.
expected_behavior: Agent indexes ADRs, identifies ADRs governing internal APIs (e.g. auth, datastore), applies adr-NNNN labels, posts one rationale comment citing each ADR's number, status, and reason.

name: tag_issue_no_matches
input: @adr Minor CSS polish on the login button hover state — change color from #123 to #456.
expected_behavior: Agent scans ADRs, finds no strong matches above threshold, posts single 'scanned N ADRs, no strong matches' comment. Does NOT apply labels.

name: tag_issue_respects_prior_removal
input: @adr (issue previously had adr-0012 label applied by the agent, then removed by a human; issue description unchanged)
expected_behavior: Agent reads memory, sees adr-0012 was previously applied and removed, does NOT re-apply it. Posts rationale for any OTHER matching ADRs.

name: review_pr_linked
input: @adr (on a PR with body 'Closes #42', diff touches auth middleware; issue #42 has labels adr-0012, adr-0019)
expected_behavior: Agent parses 'Closes #42', reads issue #42's ADR labels, evaluates the diff ONLY against ADR-0012 and ADR-0019. Posts review comments on hunks that conflict with those specific ADRs. If diff aligns, posts 'No conflicts found'.

name: review_pr_linked_no_conflicts
input: @adr (PR with 'Closes #42', diff adds tests only; linked issue has adr-0019)
expected_behavior: Agent reviews test-only diff against ADR-0019 (data layer decision), finds no conflicts, posts single 'No conflicts found' summary.

name: review_pr_unlinked_suggests_closes
input: @adr (on a PR with no Closes/Fixes/Resolves in body; diff introduces a new datastore)
expected_behavior: Agent scans the diff, infers datastore-related ADRs as candidates, posts review comments PREFIXED with [suggested], and tells the author to add 'Closes #N' for scoped review.

name: no_adr_directory
input: @adr Review this PR (in a repo with no docs/adrs, no adrs/, no ADRs/, no docs/decisions/, no architecture/decisions/)
expected_behavior: Agent posts single 'No ADR directory found' comment listing the paths it checked, does NOT hallucinate ADRs, does NOT apply labels.

name: deprecated_adr_names_successor
input: @adr (issue describes work whose best match is ADR-0005, which has 'Superseded by ADR-0042')
expected_behavior: Agent skips ADR-0005 (superseded), surfaces ADR-0042 as the applicable one. Rationale comment explicitly names the successor.

name: refuses_to_write_adr
input: @adr Write an ADR for this new datastore choice.
expected_behavior: Agent refuses. States: 'I read ADRs, I don't write them. Teams write ADRs; I can surface when your work implies a new decision.'

name: refuses_non_adr_label
input: @adr Please also add the 'needs-triage' label to this issue.
expected_behavior: Agent refuses the non-ADR label request. Only applies labels matching adr-* prefix.
